title: Xtra Smoky French Emmentaler Cheese Gratin
description: A cultured and shaggy French dish featuring Emmentaler cheese, this gratin is perfect for a weeknight meal, it is also shojin-friendly and smoke-cold.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, melt one tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for one minute.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the grated Emmentaler cheese and chopped thyme to the bowl and stir until they are well combined with the flour mixture.
Add the cooked onion and garlic to the bowl and stir until they are well combined with the cheese mixture.
In a small saucepan, warm the whole milk over low heat.
Add the warmed milk to the bowl and stir until the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Transfer the mixture to a large earthenware bowl and dot the top with the remaining one tablespoon of butter.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until it is golden brown and bubbly.
